docs.title: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul
docs.text:
EuroLeague 2025-2026  |  Scouting Report

Report Details

Prepared by: Giulia Martini. Check here to check my personal page. Date: 2026-04-15 Analysis Period: Season 2025-2026 This report is prepared analyzing the data from the EuroLeague 2025-2026 season in a programmatic way. Any plot and table are generated using the Python programming language, they are totally custom, and the source code is available on GitHub. Plots and tables can be regenerated for any team in the EuroLeague and any other season for which the data is available.

docs.title: Wade Baldwin Iv — #2
docs.text:
Stat Season Avg Last 5 Avg Points 14.4 13.6 Rebounds 3.2 2.2 Assists 5.5 5.4 FG% 45.8% 40.7% 3P% 27.7% 23.1% Minutes 26.0 27.3

Scouting Notes

  • Strengths: Explosive first step, elite in isolation and PnR; creates own shot late clock
  • Tendencies: Heavy right-hand driver; prefers going right out of high PnR; pull-up jumper after 1–2 dribbles
  • Preferred spots: Top of the key; mid-range pull-up area
  • Physicality: Very physical guard, absorbs contact well, finishes through bodies
  • Handedness: Right-handed
  • How to defend:
    • Shade him left, force weak hand
    • Show bodies early in PnR, mix coverages
    • Go under selectively (streaky shooter) but be ready to contest pull-up
    • Keep him out of rhythm, he thrives on momentum

docs.title: Devon Hall — #20
docs.text:
Stat Season Avg Last 5 Avg Points 8.6 6.2 Rebounds 3.0 3.0 Assists 2.7 2.2 FG% 41.2% 37.9% 3P% 25.8% 21.4% Minutes 24.9 22.7

Scouting Notes

  • Strengths: 3&D guard, efficient spot-up shooter, smart cutter
  • Tendencies: Plays off others; catch-and-shoot threat, attacks closeouts straight-line
  • Preferred spots: Corners, wings (especially right corner)
  • Physicality: Solid, not overly physical but strong enough to defend multiple positions
  • Handedness: Right-handed
  • How to defend:
    • No-help off him—high IQ shooter
    • Close out under control (attacks long closeouts)
    • Force him to create off the dribble

docs.title: Tarik Biberovic — #13
docs.text:
Stat Season Avg Last 5 Avg Points 10.5 11.0 Rebounds 3.1 2.7 Assists 1.3 1.3 FG% 44.1% 50.0% 3P% 39.2% 60.0% Minutes 23.8 23.0

Scouting Notes

  • Strengths: Shooting, floor spacing, off-ball movement
  • Tendencies: Catch-and-shoot, relocates well, quick trigger
  • Preferred spots: Corners and wings
  • Physicality: Not very physical; more finesse player
  • Handedness: Right-handed
  • How to defend:
    • Top-lock or chase him off screens
    • Force him to put the ball on the floor
    • Be physical with him to disrupt timing

docs.title: Talen Horton Tucker — #8
docs.text:
Stat Season Avg Last 5 Avg Points 15.7 12.4 Rebounds 3.8 4.0 Assists 1.9 2.4 FG% 48.5% 37.3% 3P% 29.0% 15.4% Minutes 23.7 25.3

Scouting Notes

  • Strengths: Strong driver, finishes with length; can create in isolation
  • Tendencies: Left-hand dominant; drives left aggressively; uses body to shield defenders
  • Preferred spots: Left wing/top; paint touches
  • Physicality: Very physical, strong frame for a guard
  • Handedness: Left-handed (important!)
  • How to defend:
    • Force right at all costs
    • Gap him slightly, make him shoot from outside
    • Show help early, he wants to get to the rim

docs.title: Nicolo Melli — #4
docs.text:
Stat Season Avg Last 5 Avg Points 6.6 8.5 Rebounds 5.4 5.0 Assists 1.6 2.5 FG% 46.4% 50.0% 3P% 41.7% 37.5% Minutes 22.7 20.6

Scouting Notes

  • Strengths: High IQ, passing, spacing (stretch big), defensive versatility
  • Tendencies: Facilitator from high post; pick-and-pop threat; quick decision-maker
  • Preferred spots: Top of the key, elbows
  • Physicality: Physical and smart defender, strong positioning
  • Handedness: Right-handed
  • How to defend:
    • Close out short but controlled
    • Deny easy passing angles, he’s a connector
    • Attack him in space with speed

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
Offensive philosophy

  • Structured, execution-heavy offense under Šarūnas Jasikevičius
  • Emphasis on fundamentals: shot selection, rebounding, low turnovers
  • Mix of half-court creation and physical play rather than pure flow

Pace

  • Generally controlled / medium pace
  • Preference to dictate tempo rather than run

Spacing principles

  • Functional spacing with multiple shooters
  • Use of stretch bigs to open driving and passing lanes
  • Strong offensive rebounding presence → accepts imperfect spacing to gain second chances

Primary ball-handlers / creators

  • Wade Baldwin IV — main creator, PnR and isolation
  • Talen Horton-Tucker — on-ball scorer, secondary creator, downhill driver
  • Devon Hall — connector, secondary playmaking
  • Nando De Colo — veteran decision-maker in half-court
  • Arturs Zagars — backup guard, organizes second unit

docs.title: Primary Offensive Entries
docs.text: Fenerbahce runs an extensive and highly structured playbook. The common thread across nearly all sets is the pick-and-roll as the primary action or continuation, with most plays designed to either create a clean PnR read for Baldwin IV or isolate Horton-Tucker in 1-on-1 situations. Jasikevičius also maintains a dedicated set of late-clock adjustments (Spain, Flare), reinforcing that this is a system built for execution and half-court control rather than improvisation.

docs.title: Entry 1 — Flat Double
docs.text:
Mianly played Horton-Tucker, sometimes by Baldwin IV

Defensive Priority

Force the ball handler to play with their weak hand, slow them down to prevent them reaching full screen and attacking the rim.

docs.title: Entry 2 — Name / Label
docs.text:
Describe the play: ball movement, screens, options, and who typically finishes.

Defensive Priority

How to stop this entry.

docs.title: Entry 3 — Spanish Pick & Roll
docs.text:
Spanish Pick and Roll between Horton-Tucker, Birch and Colson to free Colson for a uncontested 3PT shot (3PT FG >50%)

Defensive Priority

Don't switch on the first screen (Colson for Birch), defender on POA is aggressive on the ball to slow down the ball.

docs.title: Pick & Roll Offense
docs.text:
Overall PnR identity:

  • Structured, half-court heavy pick & roll system
  • Uses PnR as a read initiation tool, often flowing into second-side actions
  • Emphasis on spacing + advantage continuation rather than early-shot hunting

Who sets screens:

  • Khem Birch: primary roll big (rim pressure, vertical finishing)
  • Nicolo Melli: stretch 5/4 actions, pop + short roll playmaking
  • Mikael Jantunen: versatile screener (pop, slip, or switch-compatible screens)

Roll vs Pop tendencies:

  • Roll-heavy with Birch (rim runs, dunker spot occupation)
  • Pop/stretch heavy with Melli and Jantunen to pull bigs away from paint
  • Strong use of short roll hub actions (especially Melli as connector)

Preferred side:

  • Uses side PnR to force specific matchups, not fixed but handedness-driven advantage creation
  • Late-clock often shifts to middle PnR to neutralize the defensive opponent scheme

Ball-handler decision-making:

  • Wade Baldwin IV: primary engine, aggressive scorer + kick-out passer
  • Talen Horton-Tucker: downhill scorer, reads secondary help less than Baldwin
  • Devon Hall: connector, stabilizes possessions, avoids over-dribbling
  • Nando De Colo: late-clock manipulator, slower reads, high IQ advantage hunting

Tendencies

  • Clear handedness-driven side preference (Baldwin right / Horton-Tucker left)
  • Heavy use of PnR into second action (not first-option scoring)
  • Melli/Jantunen short roll decisions are central to offensive flow

docs.title: Base Defensive System
docs.text:
Defensive identity

  • Defense-first team, high physicality and discipline
  • Strong half-court defense; wins through control and execution
  • Emphasis on positioning, communication, and limiting mistakes

Base scheme

  • Primarily man-to-man
  • Very structured, low gambling, stay within system

Switching principles

  • Selective switching (1–3 mostly)
  • Bigger lineups allow occasional 1–4 switching
  • Avoid unnecessary mismatches vs dominant bigs

Help & rotations

  • Strong nail and low-man help
  • Good stunt-and-recover discipline
  • Prioritize protecting the paint first

Press / transition defense

  • Limited full-court press usage, used when trailing at the end of the game (see Round 35 vs Munich)
  • Focus on transition defense balance and stopping early offense

docs.title: Pick & Roll Coverage
docs.text:
Coverage Type Used Against Notes Drop Non-elite pull-up threats / secondary handlers Protect paint, rely on positioning; not primary vs top guards Hedge / Show Elite scoring guards Disrupt rhythm, force ball out, rotate behind Switch Similar size / late clock Avoid rotations; used with versatile lineups ICE / Steer Side PnR Keep ball out of middle, send to help

P&R Notes

  • Base vs top guards: Hedge/Show rather than deep drop
  • Drop is situational, not default vs elite creators
  • Coverage changes based on personnel on the floor

docs.title: Coverage Tendencies per player
docs.text:
Nicolo Melli

  • Drop → strong positioning, anchor
  • Switch → very capable (1–5 in short stretches)
  • Rarely aggressive hedge

Khem Birch

  • Drop → primary role (rim protection)
  • Limited switching
  • Occasionally contain (soft show, then recover)

Wade Baldwin IV

  • On-ball defender at POA
  • Fights over screens → enables hedge
  • Can switch 1–3

Devon Hall

  • Connector defender
  • Switch / chase over screens
  • Strong in team defense rotations

Talen Horton-Tucker

  • More physical than disciplined
  • Can switch due to size
  • Less reliable in structured coverages

Mikael Jantunen

  • Switch → comfortable 3–5, mobile big
  • Hedge/Show → capable, can move feet and recover
  • Drop → less natural; not a pure rim protector
  • Role: increases lineup mobility and switching versatility

Nando De Colo

  • Primarily off-ball defender
  • Limited switching → can be targeted physically
  • Navigates screens rather than disrupts
  • Often protected within the scheme (help behind, less exposure at POA)

docs.title: Tied or Trailing (Last 2 Minutes)
docs.text: When tied or trailing, Fenerbahce relies on their primary scorers: Horton-Tucker in 1-on-1 situations and Baldwin IV in ISO actions. Defensively, they apply on-ball pressure and double the ball-handler to force turnovers.

docs.title: Defensive Matchup Assignments
docs.text: Our Player Their Player # Notes Our best perimeter defender Wade Baldwin IV 2 Shade him left (weak hand). Mix PnR coverages — Hedge/Show primary. Contain pull-up, don't go fully under. He drives right aggressively and thrives on momentum. Our physical wing Talen Horton Tucker 8 Force right at all costs — he is left-hand dominant. Gap him slightly to discourage drives to the rim. Show help early. Our quickest wing Tarik Biberovic 13 Top-lock or chase over screens. Be physical to disrupt timing. Force him off the catch — he has a quick trigger from corners/wings. Our wing defender Devon Hall 20 No help given off him — he is a high-IQ shooter. Close out under control; he attacks long closeouts straight-line. Force creation off the dribble. Our mobile big Nicolo Melli 4 Close out short and controlled (pick-and-pop threat, 41.7% from 3). Deny easy passing angles — he is the offensive connector from the high post. Attack him in space with speed.

docs.title: Offensive Attack Strategy
docs.text: Target Fenerbahce's defensive weaknesses: their last-5-game defensive stats show significant drop-off — opponents are shooting 49.8% FG and 45.0% from three over the last 5 games (vs. season averages of 44.2% FG and 32.7% 3P%). Their transition defense also allows 9.3 fast-break points per game. The primary targets on offense are Nando De Colo (limited lateral quickness), Khem Birch (struggles in space away from the rim), and Talen Horton Tucker (inconsistent defensive discipline). Push pace early — Fenerbahce prefers a controlled, medium tempo and is more vulnerable when forced to defend in transition.

docs.title: Priority Matchups to Attack
docs.text: Priority Their Player Method 1 Nando De Colo (#12) Isolate in PnR, force switches onto him, attack downhill — limited lateral quickness is exploitable 2 Khem Birch (#92) Pick-and-pop / short roll reads — pull him away from the rim and exploit his inability to defend in space 3 Talen Horton Tucker (#8) Run multiple actions at him off-ball — test his awareness and force him into consecutive defensive reads

docs.title: Top 5 Emphasis Points
docs.text:
1 — Contain Wade Baldwin IV in Pick & Roll

Baldwin is the offensive engine (14.4 PPG, 5.5 APG). He prefers right-side PnR with Birch rolling to the rim. Use Hedge/Show coverages to disrupt his rhythm. Force him left, shade his driving lane, and mix coverages to keep him off balance. He is a streaky shooter (27.7% 3P%) — do not go fully under screens but be disciplined, not reckless.

2 — Close Out Hard on Biberovic, Never Relax

Biberovic is their most efficient 3-point shooter (39.2% on 5 attempts per game; 60% 3P% in the last 5 games). He relocates well off screens and has a quick trigger. Top-lock him on the weak side, be physical on any screen action, and force him to dribble rather than shoot.

3 — Exploit De Colo and Horton Tucker Defensively

De Colo has limited lateral quickness and is often protected within the scheme. Force him into on-ball situations via PnR or ISO. Horton Tucker can be exploited with sequential off-ball actions that test his reads. These are the two most attackable defenders in their rotation.

4 — Attack Khem Birch Away from the Rim

Birch is their primary roll man and rim protector, but he struggles when pulled out of the paint. Use pick-and-pop actions and short roll reads to draw him away from his comfort zone. With Birch out of position, the paint becomes accessible.

5 — Push Pace and Attack Transition

Fenerbahce allows 9.3 fast-break points per game and has gone 0-5 in their last 5 games. They prefer to control tempo. Generate pace off defensive rebounds and made baskets — run secondary break before their defense sets up, particularly targeting De Colo who is slow recovering. They concede 2.6 transition fouls per game leading to free throws.

docs.title: Lineup Recommendations
docs.text: Situation Lineup Reasoning Starting Best perimeter defender + mobile wing + PnR handler + stretch 4 + mobile C Need to match Fenerbahce's physical backcourt (Baldwin, THT) with length and a ball-handler who can attack De Colo and Birch vs. Their Zone Best shooters + one floor-spacing big Fenerbahce rarely uses zone, but if applied, space the floor to neutralize their physical interior presence (Birch, Bacot) Clutch Time Two-way guards + best half-court scorer + Melli matchup player Fenerbahce is 14-8 in clutch games — Horton Tucker is their primary clutch scorer (56 pts). Need defensive discipline + execution. Contain Baldwin/THT in isolation. Press Break Two ball-handlers + three outlets Fenerbahce uses full-court pressure only when trailing late. If pressed, use quick outlets, avoid turnovers (Fenerbahce forces 13.0 TOs per game in last 5 matches).

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul is a structured, defense-first team under Jasikevičius that has fallen sharply in recent form (0–5 in last 5 games, dropping from 3rd to 5th). While their season record (23-14, 62.2%) reflects genuine quality, their defense has badly deteriorated in the past month — opponents are scoring 88 points per game in this stretch and shooting 45% from three.

The three things that must be executed to win: 1) contain Wade Baldwin IV in the PnR and force him left, disrupting his rhythm before he can build momentum; 2) relentlessly attack Nando De Colo and Khem Birch in favorable matchups — these are the two most exploitable defenders in their scheme; 3) push tempo every chance available, denying Fenerbahce the controlled, half-court game that is their comfort zone. This team is winnable against right now — their confidence is low and their defense has significant cracks. Execute the game plan, attack the matchups, and take care of the ball against their pressure.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Beyond standard team and player profiling, additional quantitative analysis can be conducted to support scouting, game preparation, and in-game adjustments. These metrics focus on lineups interaction, player synergy, and high-leverage possessions, providing deeper insight into performance beyond traditional box score indicators.

docs.title: Lineups Analysis
docs.text:
Evaluation of five-man units to identify the most effective and most vulnerable combinations.

Key outputs: - Offensive Rating / Defensive Rating per lineup - Net Rating (on-court impact) - Usage of starting vs bench units - Lineup stability under different game contexts - Spacing efficiency and shot quality by unit

docs.title: Duo Analysis
docs.text:
Assessment of two-player combinations to evaluate on-court synergy.

Key outputs: - Net Rating for player pairs - Assist connections between primary creators and finishers - Pick-and-roll efficiency by specific duo - On/off shooting splits when paired - Defensive compatibility in switch and hedge schemes

docs.title: Clutch Time Analysis
docs.text:
Performance in end-game situations (last 4 minutes, score < 10 points).

Key outputs: - Clutch Offensive and Defensive Rating - Primary shot creators in late possessions - Shot distribution changes under pressure - Turnover rate in high-pressure possessions - Free throw generation and efficiency in close games

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: These advanced metrics complement standard scouting by focusing on interaction effects and situational performance, supporting lineup optimization, rotation decisions, and end-game strategy.
